title: sticherus oceanicus
parts: Sticherus oceanicus has many medicinal parts, such as leaves, roots, and stems. The leaves of Sticherus oceanicus contain flavonoids and phenolic acids, which exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The roots of Sticherus oceanicus are rich in sesquiterpenoids and alkaloids, which have been found to have antimicrobial and antiviral activities. The stems of Sticherus oceanicus are also a source of medicinal constituents, including glycosides and terpenoids, which may possess anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties.
side_effects: Sticherus oceanicus can have side effects if used improperly, such as inducing allergic reactions, respiratory issues, or gastrointestinal problems in some individuals. Prolonged exposure to its extracts can lead to dizziness, headaches, and nausea, while high doses may cause liver damage or kidney strain. Additionally, improper use of the plant can cause skin irritation, rashes, or itchiness due to its toxic compounds. In severe cases, misuse of Sticherus oceanicus can even result in anaphylaxis, a life-threatening allergic reaction that requires immediate medical attention.
